steps.title: Identify the subject and tense context
steps.content: The subject is "Ann Greely" — one person, singular. The phrase "in the late 1800s" establishes a past time context. The verb must be singular and past tense.

steps.title: Rule: singular past-tense subject takes "was," not "were"
steps.content:
Per-choice diagnosis:

A. "was" — singular past-tense verb. Ann Greely was a fierce advocate — agrees in both number and tense. Correct.

B. "have been" — plural present-perfect; disagrees in both number (plural) and conflicts with "late 1800s" past context. Incorrect.

C. "are" — plural present-tense; disagrees in number and tense. Incorrect.

D. "were" — past-tense but plural (used with they/you); Ann Greely is singular. Incorrect.

A is correct.
